epics.title: Core Editor
epics.description: The fundamental text editing engine that supports multi-file editing with full cursor control, text manipulation, and buffer management.

user_stories.title: Text Buffer Management
acceptance_criteria: Given a newly created buffer, when text is inserted at row 0 col 0, then the buffer contains that text and LineCount reflects the correct number of lines
Given a buffer with content "hello world", when text is inserted at row 0 col 5, then the line reads "hello world" split at the insertion with the new text between
Given a buffer with two lines, when a delete spans from the end of line 1 to the start of line 2, then the two lines merge into one
Given a file with CRLF line endings, when loaded into a buffer, then all line endings are normalized to LF and LineCount matches the original line count
Given a file with mixed CR and CRLF endings, when loaded into a buffer, then every line break is treated as a single LF boundary
Given a buffer with unmodified content, when Modified is queried, then it returns false
Given a buffer where text has been inserted, when Modified is queried, then it returns true
Given a modified buffer, when SetModified(false) is called after save, then Modified returns false
technical_notes: Current implementation uses a line-based string slice with undo stack
Consider migrating to piece table or rope for large files in the future

user_stories.title: Undo and Redo
acceptance_criteria: Given the user types "hello" into an empty buffer, when Ctrl+Z is pressed, then the buffer is empty and the cursor returns to row 0 col 0
Given the user deletes a word, when Ctrl+Z is pressed, then the deleted word reappears at its original position and the cursor returns to before the delete
Given the user has pressed Ctrl+Z three times, when Ctrl+Y is pressed once, then the most recently undone operation is re-applied and the cursor moves to its post-edit position
Given the user undoes two operations then types a new character, when Ctrl+Y is pressed, then nothing happens because the redo stack was cleared by the new edit
Given an empty undo stack, when Ctrl+Z is pressed, then nothing happens and the buffer remains unchanged
Given an empty redo stack, when Ctrl+Y is pressed, then nothing happens and the buffer remains unchanged
Given a multi-line insertion is undone, when the undo completes, then all inserted lines are removed and the cursor returns to the original insertion point
technical_notes: Operations stored as EditOp structs with type, text, and cursor positions
Redo stack cleared on any new edit to maintain linear history

user_stories.title: Cursor Movement
acceptance_criteria: Given the cursor is at row 0 col 5, when the Left arrow is pressed, then the cursor moves to row 0 col 4
Given the cursor is at row 1 col 0, when the Left arrow is pressed, then the cursor moves to the end of row 0
Given the cursor is at the end of row 0, when the Right arrow is pressed, then the cursor moves to row 1 col 0
Given the cursor is at row 3 col 10, when the Up arrow is pressed, then the cursor moves to row 2 col 10 or the end of row 2 if it is shorter
Given the cursor is at row 0 col 5, when Home is pressed, then the cursor moves to row 0 col 0
Given the cursor is at row 0 col 0, when End is pressed, then the cursor moves to the last column of row 0
Given a document with 100 lines, when Ctrl+Home is pressed, then the cursor moves to row 0 col 0
Given a document with 100 lines, when Ctrl+End is pressed, then the cursor moves to the last row and last column of the document
Given the cursor is at col 10 in "hello world", when Ctrl+Left is pressed, then the cursor jumps to col 6 (start of "world")
Given the cursor is at col 0 in "hello world", when Ctrl+Right is pressed, then the cursor jumps to col 5 (end of "hello" / start of space)
Given a visible page height of 20 lines and the cursor at row 10, when Page Down is pressed, then the cursor moves to row 29 or the last line if fewer lines exist

user_stories.title: Text Selection
acceptance_criteria: Given the cursor is at row 0 col 5 with no selection, when Shift+Right is pressed, then a selection is created from (0,5) to (0,6) and the character at col 5 is visually highlighted
Given an active selection from (0,5) to (0,8), when Shift+Left is pressed, then the selection shrinks to (0,5) to (0,7)
Given an active selection from (0,5) to (0,8), when the Right arrow is pressed without Shift, then the selection is cleared and the cursor is at col 8
Given a 50-line document, when Ctrl+A is pressed, then the entire document is selected from (0,0) to the last character of the last line
Given text "world" is selected, when the user types "earth", then "world" is deleted and "earth" is inserted in its place as a single undoable operation
Given text "foo" is selected and the user presses Shift+Down, then the selection extends from the start of "foo" down to the corresponding column on the next line

user_stories.title: Clipboard Integration
acceptance_criteria: Given text "hello" is selected, when Ctrl+C is pressed, then "hello" is placed on the OS clipboard and the selection remains active in the editor
Given text "hello" is selected, when Ctrl+X is pressed, then "hello" is placed on the OS clipboard and the selected text is removed from the buffer
Given the OS clipboard contains "world" and the cursor is at row 0 col 5, when Ctrl+V is pressed, then "world" is inserted at that position and the cursor advances past it
Given text "old" is selected and the clipboard contains "new", when Ctrl+V is pressed, then "old" is replaced by "new" in a single undoable operation
Given the application is running on macOS, when Ctrl+C is pressed with a selection, then pbcopy receives the selected text via stdin
Given the application is running on Linux with xclip installed, when Ctrl+V is pressed, then the text from xclip -selection clipboard -o is inserted at the cursor
technical_notes: Uses os/exec to shell out to platform clipboard commands

user_stories.title: Multi-Tab Editing
acceptance_criteria: Given no tabs are open, when Ctrl+N is pressed, then a new tab labeled "untitled" appears in the tab bar and becomes the active tab
Given tabs "main.go" and "util.go" are open with "main.go" active, when the user clicks the "util.go" tab, then "util.go" becomes active and its buffer and cursor position are displayed
Given three tabs are open with tab 2 active, when Ctrl+3 is pressed, then tab 3 becomes active
Given "main.go" is already open in tab 1, when the user opens "main.go" again via File Open, then the editor switches to tab 1 instead of creating a duplicate tab
Given the active tab has unsaved changes, when Ctrl+W is pressed, then a confirmation dialog asks whether to save before closing
Given the active tab has no unsaved changes, when Ctrl+W is pressed, then the tab closes immediately without a prompt
Given a tab has been modified, when the tab bar is rendered, then the tab name shows an asterisk prefix like "*main.go"
Given tab 2 is active with cursor at row 10 col 5, when the user switches to tab 1 and back to tab 2, then the cursor is still at row 10 col 5 and the scroll position is preserved

user_stories.title: Basic Text Editing Operations
acceptance_criteria: Given an empty buffer with the cursor at row 0 col 0, when the user types "a", then the buffer contains "a" and the cursor is at row 0 col 1
Given the buffer contains "hllo" with the cursor at row 0 col 1, when the user types "e", then the buffer contains "hello" and the cursor is at row 0 col 2
Given the buffer contains "hello world" with the cursor at row 0 col 5, when Enter is pressed, then line 0 becomes "hello", line 1 becomes " world", and the cursor is at row 1 col 0
Given the buffer contains "hello" with the cursor at row 0 col 3, when Backspace is pressed, then the buffer contains "helo" and the cursor is at row 0 col 2
Given two lines "hello" and "world" with the cursor at row 1 col 0, when Backspace is pressed, then the lines merge to "helloworld" and the cursor is at row 0 col 5
Given the buffer contains "hello" with the cursor at row 0 col 3, when Delete is pressed, then the buffer contains "helo" and the cursor stays at row 0 col 3
Given two lines "hello" and "world" with the cursor at the end of row 0, when Delete is pressed, then the lines merge to "helloworld"
Given the cursor is at col 0, when Tab is pressed, then four space characters are inserted and the cursor moves to col 4
Given a three-line buffer with the cursor on line 2, when Ctrl+D is pressed, then line 2 is deleted and the buffer has two lines

epics.title: Syntax Highlighting
epics.description: Language-aware colorization of source code using lexical analysis to visually distinguish keywords, strings, comments, and other token types.

user_stories.title: Language Detection
acceptance_criteria: Given a file named "main.go" is opened, when the highlighter runs, then the detected language is "Go" and the status bar shows "Go"
Given a file named "app.py" is opened, when the highlighter runs, then the detected language is "Python"
Given a file named "styles.css" is opened, when the highlighter runs, then the detected language is "CSS"
Given a file named "README.txt" is opened, when the highlighter runs, then the language falls back to "Text" and no syntax coloring is applied
Given a file named "main.c" is saved as "main.py" via Save As, when the save completes, then the language re-detects as "Python" and highlighting updates accordingly
Given a file is open with language "Go" detected, when the status bar renders, then it displays "Go" in the language section

user_stories.title: Token Colorization
acceptance_criteria: Given a Go file containing "func main()", when rendered, then "func" appears in white bold and "main" appears in white bold as a function name
Given a Python file containing 'print("hello")', when rendered, then "hello" including quotes appears in cyan
Given a C file containing "// comment", when rendered, then the entire comment line appears in gray
Given a Rust file containing "let x = 42;", when rendered, then "42" appears in magenta
Given a Go file containing "type Server struct", when rendered, then "type" appears in white bold and "Server" appears in green
Given a JavaScript file containing a multi-line block comment, when rendered, then all lines of the comment appear in gray
Given a C++ file containing '#include <stdio.h>', when rendered, then the preprocessor directive is colored distinctly from regular code
technical_notes: Uses chroma library for lexical analysis
Per-character CharStyle array cached per line, invalidated on edit
Consider tree-sitter migration for structural accuracy in the future

user_stories.title: Highlighting Performance
acceptance_criteria: Given a file is open and no edits have occurred since the last render, when Draw is called, then the cached highlight data is reused without re-tokenizing
Given the user types a character in the buffer, when the highlight version counter is checked, then it has incremented by one since the last edit
Given the user switches from tab 1 to tab 2, when Draw is called, then the highlight cache is invalidated and tab 2's content is tokenized fresh
Given a file with 5000 lines, when the user types a character, then the keystroke is processed and the screen redraws within 100 milliseconds
Given a file containing a block comment spanning 50 lines, when rendered, then all 50 lines of the comment are colored gray because the tokenizer processes the full file

epics.title: File Management
epics.description: All operations related to opening, saving, browsing, and managing files and directories within the IDE.

user_stories.title: File Open
acceptance_criteria: Given the user presses Ctrl+O, when the dialog appears, then it shows the current working directory contents with directories sorted before files
Given the open dialog shows "/Users/me/project", when the user clicks a subdirectory "src", then the dialog navigates into "src" and shows its contents
Given the open dialog shows files, when the user selects "main.go", then "main.go" opens in a new editor tab and the dialog closes
Given the open dialog path input field, when the user types "/tmp" and presses Enter, then the dialog navigates to /tmp and lists its contents
Given a directory containing ".git" and ".env" hidden entries, when the open dialog lists files, then the hidden entries are not shown
Given the open dialog is showing, when the user presses Escape, then the dialog closes without opening any file and focus returns to the editor

user_stories.title: File Save
acceptance_criteria: Given a tab editing "/tmp/test.go" with modifications, when Ctrl+S is pressed, then the file at "/tmp/test.go" is overwritten with the buffer contents and the modified flag clears
Given an untitled tab with no file path, when Ctrl+S is pressed, then a Save As dialog appears prompting for a file path
Given a tab editing a file in a read-only directory, when Ctrl+S is pressed, then an error message appears in the output panel and the buffer content is not lost
Given a successful save of "/tmp/test.go", when the save completes, then the status bar displays "File saved: /tmp/test.go"
Given a tab with no modifications, when Ctrl+S is pressed, then the file is saved anyway and the status bar confirms the save

user_stories.title: Save As
acceptance_criteria: Given a tab editing "/tmp/old.go", when Save As is triggered, then the dialog shows an input field pre-filled with "/tmp/old.go"
Given an untitled tab, when Save As is triggered, then the dialog shows an input field pre-filled with the working directory plus "/untitled"
Given the Save As dialog with path "/tmp/new.py", when the user confirms, then the file is written to "/tmp/new.py", the tab name updates to "new.py", and the language re-detects as Python
Given a successful Save As to "/tmp/new.py", when the config is checked, then "/tmp/new.py" appears in the recent files list

user_stories.title: File Tree Browser
acceptance_criteria: Given the application launches in "/Users/me/project", when the file tree renders, then it shows "project" as the root node with its immediate children listed
Given the file tree shows a collapsed directory "src", when the user clicks "src", then it expands to show its children files and subdirectories
Given the file tree shows an expanded directory "src", when the user clicks "src" again, then it collapses and hides its children
Given the file tree shows "main.go", when the user clicks it, then "main.go" opens in a new editor tab or the existing tab is activated
Given a Go file "main.go" in the tree, when rendered, then it shows a "g" type indicator before the filename
Given the user selects Tools > Refresh File Tree, when files have been added externally, then the tree reloads and shows the new files

user_stories.title: New File
acceptance_criteria: Given no tabs are open, when Ctrl+N is pressed, then a new tab labeled "untitled" appears with an empty buffer and the cursor at row 0 col 0
Given two tabs are open, when Ctrl+N is pressed, then a third tab "untitled" is added and becomes the active tab with focus
Given one untitled tab exists, when Ctrl+N is pressed again, then a second untitled tab is created without a naming conflict

user_stories.title: Recent Files
acceptance_criteria: Given the user opens "/tmp/a.go" then "/tmp/b.py", when the config is checked, then the recent files list starts with "/tmp/b.py" followed by "/tmp/a.go"
Given 21 files have been opened, when the recent files list is checked, then it contains only the 20 most recent paths
Given "/tmp/a.go" is already in the recent files list at position 5, when "/tmp/a.go" is opened again, then it moves to position 1 without duplication
Given the File menu is opened, when the Recent Files submenu is displayed, then it lists recent paths with the most recent first
Given a recent file "/tmp/old.go" is selected from the menu, when the file still exists, then it opens in a new tab or switches to its existing tab
technical_notes: Duplicates removed, list capped at 20 entries

epics.title: Search and Navigation
epics.description: Finding text, replacing text, and navigating to specific locations within the editor buffer.

user_stories.title: Find
acceptance_criteria: Given the user presses Ctrl+F, when the dialog appears, then it contains a text input field labeled "Search" and a "Find Next" button
Given the buffer contains "hello world hello" with the cursor at col 0, when the user searches for "hello", then the cursor moves to col 0 and the first "hello" (col 0-4) is selected
Given the first "hello" is already selected, when Find Next is pressed again, then the cursor moves to the second "hello" and selects it
Given the cursor is past the last occurrence, when Find Next is pressed, then the search wraps to the beginning and finds the first occurrence
Given the buffer contains "Hello" and the user searches for "hello", when Find Next is pressed, then "Hello" is found because search is case-insensitive
Given the buffer does not contain "xyz", when the user searches for "xyz", then the status bar displays "Not found: xyz"
Given the find dialog is open, when the user presses Escape, then the dialog closes and focus returns to the editor

user_stories.title: Replace
acceptance_criteria: Given the user presses Ctrl+H, when the dialog appears, then it shows Find and Replace input fields plus Find, Replace, and Replace All buttons
Given "hello" is found and selected, when the user enters "world" in Replace and clicks Replace, then "hello" is replaced with "world" and the search advances to the next "hello"
Given a buffer with 5 occurrences of "foo", when Replace All is clicked with find "foo" and replace "bar", then all 5 are replaced and the status bar shows "Replaced 5 occurrences"
Given a Replace All operation, when Ctrl+Z is pressed, then all replacements are undone in a single undo step restoring the original text
Given no match is currently selected, when Replace is clicked, then the system first performs a Find before attempting replacement

user_stories.title: Go to Line
acceptance_criteria: Given the user presses Ctrl+G, when the dialog appears, then it shows a numeric input field labeled "Line number"
Given a 100-line document, when the user enters "50" and confirms, then the cursor moves to the beginning of line 50 and the view scrolls to show it
Given a 100-line document, when the user enters "200" and confirms, then the cursor moves to line 100 (the last line) without an error
Given the user enters "0" or a negative number, when confirmed, then the cursor moves to line 1 without an error
Given the Go to Line dialog is open, when the user presses Escape, then the dialog closes without moving the cursor

epics.title: Build and Run System
epics.description: Compiling source code and executing programs with output captured and displayed within the IDE.

user_stories.title: Language Build Commands
acceptance_criteria: Given a file "main.c" is active, when the build system detects the language, then it selects "gcc" with flags "-Wall" as the build command
Given a file "app.cpp" is active, when the build system detects the language, then it selects "g++" with flags "-Wall -std=c++17"
Given a file "main.rs" is active, when the build system detects the language, then it selects "rustc" as the build command
Given a file "main.go" is active, when the build system detects the language, then it selects "go build" as the build command
Given a file "script.py" is active, when the build system checks for a build step, then it returns nil indicating no compilation is needed
Given a file "app.js" is active, when the build system selects the run command, then it chooses "node app.js"
Given a file "app.ts" is active, when the build system selects the run command, then it chooses "npx tsx app.ts"
Given a file "Main.java" is active, when the build system detects the language, then it selects "javac" for build and "java -cp <dir> Main" for run

user_stories.title: Run Execution
acceptance_criteria: Given a file "main.go" with valid code, when F5 is pressed, then the file is saved, "go run main.go" executes, and stdout appears in the output panel
Given a file "main.c" with valid code, when F9 is pressed, then the file is saved, gcc compiles it, "Build successful" appears, but the program does not execute
Given a file "main.c" with a syntax error, when F5 is pressed, then gcc output shows the error in the output panel and the program does not execute
Given a running process, when the output panel renders, then it shows the command in cyan, output in white, and the exit code with elapsed time on completion
Given a program that runs for 35 seconds, when the timeout is reached at 30 seconds, then the process is killed and "timeout" appears in the output panel
Given a build and run is in progress, when the user types in the editor, then keystrokes are processed immediately without waiting for the build to complete

user_stories.title: Process Control
acceptance_criteria: Given a process is running, when the user selects Run > Stop, then the process is terminated and the output panel shows "Process stopped"
Given no process is running, when Run > Stop is selected, then nothing happens and no error is displayed
Given a process is running, when F5 is pressed again, then the existing process is terminated before the new run starts

user_stories.title: Output Panel
acceptance_criteria: Given the application layout is rendered, when the output panel is visible, then it occupies the bottom 8 rows of the screen
Given a build command "gcc main.c" is executed, when the command starts, then "> gcc main.c" appears in cyan in the output panel
Given a compilation error occurs, when the error text is appended, then it appears in red in the output panel
Given a successful build, when the success message is appended, then it appears in green
Given 100 lines of output, when new output is appended, then the panel auto-scrolls to show the latest line
Given the output panel has content, when Tools > Clear Output is selected, then the output panel becomes empty

epics.title: User Interface
epics.description: The visual layout, menus, status indicators, and dialog system that provide the IDE's interactive surface.

user_stories.title: Application Layout
acceptance_criteria: Given the application starts, when the main window renders, then the menu bar occupies row 0, the file tree occupies 20 columns on the left, the editor fills the remaining center space, the output panel occupies the bottom 8 rows, and the status bar occupies the last row
Given a terminal window 80 columns wide, when the layout renders, then the file tree is 20 columns and the editor is 60 columns wide
Given the application starts, when the background is drawn, then the editor area and file tree use dark blue (#000080) and the menu and status bars use cyan
Given the application starts, when mouse events are checked, then tview.EnableMouse has been called and click events are delivered to components

user_stories.title: Menu Bar
acceptance_criteria: Given the application is running, when the menu bar renders, then it shows labels: File, Edit, Search, Run, Tools, Options, Window, Help
Given no menu is open, when the user clicks "File", then a dropdown appears showing New, Open, Save, Save As, Close Tab, and Exit with their shortcuts
Given the File dropdown is open, when the user presses Right arrow, then the File dropdown closes and the Edit dropdown opens
Given the Edit dropdown is open with "Undo" highlighted, when the user presses Down arrow, then "Redo" becomes highlighted
Given the File dropdown is open with "Save" highlighted, when the user presses Enter, then the save action executes and the dropdown closes
Given a dropdown is open, when the user presses Escape, then the dropdown closes and focus returns to the editor
Given no menu is open, when F10 is pressed, then the File menu opens and the menu bar receives focus
Given the Run dropdown is open, when the user clicks outside the dropdown area, then the dropdown closes without executing any action

user_stories.title: Status Bar
acceptance_criteria: Given a file "main.go" is open with cursor at row 10 col 5, when the status bar renders, then it displays "main.go | Ln 11, Col 6 | Go | UTF-8"
Given the file "main.go" has been modified, when the status bar renders, then it shows "main.go [modified] | Ln 11, Col 6 | Go | UTF-8"
Given the status bar is rendered, when the right side is checked, then it shows "F5:Run  F9:Build  F10:Menu"
Given no tabs are open, when the status bar renders, then it displays "NumenText" with a hint to open or create files

user_stories.title: Modal Dialogs
acceptance_criteria: Given the user presses Ctrl+F, when the find dialog appears, then it renders centered on the screen overlaying the editor
Given a dialog is open, when a key is pressed, then only the dialog receives the key event and the editor does not process it
Given the find dialog is open, when Escape is pressed, then the dialog disappears and focus returns to the editor
Given a tab with unsaved changes, when Ctrl+W is pressed, then a confirmation dialog appears with the message "Save changes to <filename>?" and Yes/No buttons
Given a dialog is showing, when input fields are rendered, then they use blue background with white text matching the theme

user_stories.title: Welcome Screen
acceptance_criteria: Given no tabs are open, when the editor area renders, then the NumenText ASCII art logo is displayed centered in the available space
Given the welcome screen is shown, when the user looks at the content, then it lists shortcuts: Ctrl+N, Ctrl+O, Ctrl+S, Ctrl+Q, F5, F9, Ctrl+F, Ctrl+G, F10
Given the welcome screen is shown, when the text is read, then it includes "A Modern Terminal IDE" and "Inspired by Borland C++"
Given no tabs are open, when the user presses Ctrl+N, then the welcome screen is replaced by the new empty tab

user_stories.title: Color Theme
acceptance_criteria: Given the application renders, when the editor background is checked, then it uses dark blue (#000080)
Given the application renders, when the menu bar colors are checked, then it uses white or black text on a cyan background
Given text is selected in the editor, when the selection renders, then selected characters show as black text on cyan background
Given two tabs are open with tab 1 active, when the tab bar renders, then tab 1 shows white text on blue and tab 2 shows gray text on darker blue
Given a Go file is open, when keywords like "func" render, then they appear in white bold; strings in cyan; comments in gray; numbers in magenta

epics.title: Configuration and Persistence
epics.description: Saving user preferences, application state, and settings across sessions.

user_stories.title: Configuration File
acceptance_criteria: Given no ~/.numentext directory exists, when the application saves config for the first time, then the directory is created and config.json is written inside it
Given ~/.numentext/config.json exists with valid JSON, when the application starts, then the config values are loaded and applied
Given ~/.numentext/config.json is corrupted or contains invalid JSON, when the application starts, then default values are used silently without displaying an error
Given the user opens a file, when the config is saved, then the recent_files array in config.json includes the new file path
Given the config file is read, when its contents are checked, then it includes keys for tab_size, theme, show_line_numbers, word_wrap, and recent_files

user_stories.title: Preference Options
acceptance_criteria: Given line numbers are currently visible, when the user selects Options > Toggle Line Numbers, then the gutter disappears and the editor content fills the freed space
Given line numbers are currently hidden, when the user selects Options > Toggle Line Numbers, then the gutter reappears with line numbers displayed
Given the user toggles line numbers off, when the application is restarted, then line numbers remain off because the preference was persisted to config.json
Given a preference is changed, when the change takes effect, then the UI updates immediately without requiring a restart

epics.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
epics.description: Comprehensive keyboard shortcut system providing efficient access to all major IDE functions.

user_stories.title: Global Shortcuts
acceptance_criteria: Given the file tree has focus, when Ctrl+N is pressed, then a new tab is created because Ctrl+N is a global shortcut
Given the output panel has focus, when Ctrl+S is pressed, then the current file saves because Ctrl+S is a global shortcut
Given the file tree has focus, when F5 is pressed, then the current file runs because F5 is a global shortcut
Given the editor has focus, when F10 is pressed, then the menu bar opens with the File menu active
Given a dialog is open, when Escape is pressed, then the dialog closes; given no dialog is open and the file tree has focus, when Escape is pressed, then focus returns to the editor
Given a dropdown menu is open, when Escape is pressed, then the menu closes and focus returns to the editor

user_stories.title: Editor Shortcuts
acceptance_criteria: Given the editor has focus and text has been typed, when Ctrl+Z is pressed, then the last edit is undone
Given the editor has focus and an undo has been performed, when Ctrl+Y is pressed, then the undo is reversed
Given text "hello" is selected, when Ctrl+X is pressed, then "hello" is cut to the clipboard and removed from the buffer
Given text "hello" is selected, when Ctrl+C is pressed, then "hello" is copied to the clipboard and remains in the buffer
Given the clipboard contains "world", when Ctrl+V is pressed, then "world" is inserted at the cursor
Given the editor has focus, when Ctrl+A is pressed, then all text in the buffer is selected
Given the cursor is on line 5, when Ctrl+D is pressed, then line 5 is deleted
Given three tabs are open with tab 1 active, when Ctrl+Tab is pressed, then tab 2 becomes active

user_stories.title: Tab Switching
acceptance_criteria: Given tabs "a.go", "b.py", "c.rs" are open, when Ctrl+2 is pressed, then "b.py" becomes the active tab
Given tabs "a.go" and "b.py" are open, when Ctrl+5 is pressed, then nothing happens because tab 5 does not exist
Given three tabs with tab 3 active, when Ctrl+Tab is pressed, then tab 1 becomes active because it wraps around

epics.title: Mouse Support
epics.description: Full mouse interaction for cursor placement, text selection, scrolling, and UI navigation.

user_stories.title: Editor Mouse Interaction
acceptance_criteria: Given a file is open with content visible, when the user left-clicks at screen position corresponding to row 5 col 10, then the cursor moves to row 5 col 10 and any selection is cleared
Given a file with 200 lines is open and scrolled to line 50, when the user scrolls the mouse wheel down, then the view scrolls down by 3 lines to show line 53 at the top
Given a file with 200 lines scrolled to line 10, when the user scrolls the mouse wheel up, then the view scrolls up by 3 lines to show line 7 at the top
Given tabs "main.go" and "util.go" are shown in the tab bar, when the user clicks on "util.go" in the tab bar, then "util.go" becomes the active tab

user_stories.title: File Tree Mouse Interaction
acceptance_criteria: Given the file tree shows "main.go", when the user left-clicks on "main.go", then the file opens in the editor in a new tab or the existing tab activates
Given the file tree shows a collapsed directory "src", when the user left-clicks on "src", then the directory expands to show its children
Given the file tree shows an expanded directory "src", when the user left-clicks on "src" again, then the directory collapses
Given the file tree has more entries than visible rows, when the user scrolls the mouse wheel in the file tree area, then the tree view scrolls

user_stories.title: Menu Mouse Interaction
acceptance_criteria: Given the menu bar shows "File Edit Search", when the user left-clicks on "Edit", then the Edit dropdown menu opens
Given the Edit dropdown is open, when the user left-clicks on "File" in the menu bar, then the Edit dropdown closes and the File dropdown opens
Given the File dropdown is open showing "New, Open, Save", when the user clicks "Open", then the open file action triggers and the dropdown closes
Given the File dropdown is open, when the user clicks on the editor area outside the dropdown, then the dropdown closes without executing any action

epics.title: Cross-Platform and Deployment
epics.description: Ensuring the IDE runs reliably across operating systems and can be easily deployed to remote machines.

user_stories.title: Single Binary Distribution
acceptance_criteria: Given the source code, when "go build -o numentext ." is executed, then a single binary is produced with no dynamically linked dependencies other than libc
Given a macOS build machine, when "GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build -o numentext-linux ." is executed, then a Linux binary is produced that runs on x86_64 Linux machines
Given a compiled binary, when it is copied to a remote server via scp and executed, then it starts without errors and with no prior installation or configuration

user_stories.title: Platform Compatibility
acceptance_criteria: Given the application runs in iTerm2 on macOS, when the UI renders, then all panels, menus, and text display correctly without rendering artifacts
Given the application runs in GNOME Terminal on Linux, when the UI renders, then all panels, menus, and text display correctly
Given the application runs in Alacritty, when the UI renders, then all characters display correctly because only ASCII is used for UI elements
Given the application starts on macOS, when clipboard copy is invoked, then it uses "pbcopy"; given Linux, then it uses "xclip -selection clipboard"

epics.title: Embedded Terminal
epics.description: A real interactive terminal emulator embedded within the IDE for running shell commands without leaving the editor.

user_stories.title: PTY Integration
acceptance_criteria: Given the embedded terminal is activated, when the terminal starts, then a pseudo-terminal is spawned running the user's default shell (from SHELL environment variable)
Given the terminal is running bash, when the user types "ls" and presses Enter, then the directory listing appears in the terminal panel
Given the terminal is running, when an interactive program like "top" is launched, then ANSI escape sequences are interpreted and the screen renders correctly
Given the terminal is running, when the user switches to another editor tab and back, then the terminal session state, scroll position, and shell history are preserved
Given the terminal panel is resized, when the resize completes, then SIGWINCH is sent to the PTY and the shell adjusts its column and row count
technical_notes: Need a VT100/ANSI state machine to parse escape sequences into a cell grid
Render the cell grid using tcell like the editor does

user_stories.title: Terminal Panel
acceptance_criteria: Given the application starts, when the output panel is rendered, then there is an option or keybinding to toggle between output-only mode and terminal mode
Given the terminal mode is active, when the user presses the toggle shortcut, then the panel switches to output-only mode showing build/run results
Given the terminal is active, when the user presses the shortcut to focus the editor, then focus moves to the editor without killing the terminal process
Given the user runs a build via F5, when terminal mode is active, then the build output optionally goes to the terminal allowing interactive program input
Given a terminal session has produced 1500 lines of output, when the user scrolls up, then at least the last 1000 lines are available in the scrollback buffer

epics.title: Language Server Protocol
epics.description: Integration with LSP servers to provide intelligent code assistance including completions, diagnostics, and navigation.

user_stories.title: LSP Client Core
acceptance_criteria: Given a Go file is opened and gopls is installed, when the LSP client initializes, then it spawns gopls, sends the initialize request, and receives the server's capabilities
Given the LSP client has initialized, when the user opens a file, then a textDocument/didOpen notification is sent with the file URI and contents
Given the user edits a file, when a character is typed, then a textDocument/didChange notification is sent with the incremental change
Given the language server process crashes, when the client detects the broken pipe, then it displays "Language server crashed" in the status bar and allows restart via the Tools menu
Given a Python file is opened and pyright is installed, when the LSP client initializes, then it launches pyright and completes the handshake
Given a file type with no known language server, when the file is opened, then no LSP client is started and the editor functions normally without code intelligence
technical_notes: Common servers: gopls (Go), clangd (C/C++), pyright (Python), rust-analyzer (Rust), typescript-language-server (JS/TS)
Consider using an existing Go LSP client library rather than implementing the protocol from scratch

user_stories.title: Autocomplete
acceptance_criteria: Given a Go file with "fmt." typed, when the dot trigger character is entered, then a completion popup appears listing Printf, Println, Sprintf, and other fmt members
Given the completion popup is visible with 5 items, when the user presses Down arrow twice, then the third item is highlighted
Given the completion popup shows "Println" highlighted, when the user presses Enter, then "Println" is inserted at the cursor and the popup closes
Given the completion popup is visible, when the user presses Escape, then the popup closes without inserting anything
Given the completion popup is visible, when the user continues typing "Pr", then the list filters to show only completions starting with "Pr"
Given a Python file with "os.path." typed, when the dot is entered, then completions for os.path members appear

user_stories.title: Diagnostics
acceptance_criteria: Given a Go file with an undefined variable "x", when the language server responds with diagnostics, then a red marker appears in the gutter on the line containing "x"
Given a Python file with an unused import, when the language server sends a warning diagnostic, then a yellow marker appears in the gutter
Given the cursor moves to a line with a diagnostic, when the line is focused, then the diagnostic message (e.g., "undefined: x") displays in the status bar
Given multiple diagnostics exist, when the output panel summary is checked, then it lists each diagnostic with filename, line number, and message
Given a diagnostic exists on line 10, when the user fixes the error and the language server sends updated diagnostics without line 10, then the red marker on line 10 disappears

user_stories.title: Go to Definition
acceptance_criteria: Given the cursor is on a function call "DoWork()" in a Go file, when the go-to-definition shortcut is pressed, then the cursor jumps to the line where DoWork is defined
Given the definition of "DoWork" is in "worker.go", when go-to-definition is triggered, then "worker.go" opens in a new tab with the cursor at the definition line
Given the definition is 100 lines below in the same file, when go-to-definition completes, then the cursor moves to that line and the view scrolls to center it
Given the cursor is on a variable with no definition available, when go-to-definition is triggered, then the status bar displays "No definition found"

user_stories.title: Hover Information
acceptance_criteria: Given the cursor is on a function name "Printf" in a Go file, when the hover shortcut is pressed, then a popup appears showing "func fmt.Printf(format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)"
Given the cursor is on a type name "Server", when the hover shortcut is pressed, then a popup shows the type definition and any documentation comments
Given the hover popup is visible, when the user presses Escape, then the popup closes
Given the hover popup is visible, when the user moves the cursor to a different line, then the popup closes
Given the hover popup is visible, when the user starts typing, then the popup closes and the typed character is inserted normally

epics.title: Debug Adapter Protocol
epics.description: Integration with DAP servers to provide interactive debugging with breakpoints, stepping, and variable inspection.

user_stories.title: DAP Client Core
acceptance_criteria: Given a Go file is active and dlv (Delve) is installed, when a debug session is started, then the DAP client launches Delve in DAP mode and completes the initialize/launch handshake
Given a Python file is active and debugpy is installed, when a debug session is started, then debugpy launches and the session initializes
Given the debug adapter crashes during a session, when the DAP client detects the failure, then it displays "Debug adapter disconnected" in the status bar and cleans up the session
Given a launch request is sent, when the adapter responds with success, then the program begins execution and the status bar shows "Debugging"
Given an attach request is configured, when the session starts, then the DAP client connects to the running process instead of launching a new one
technical_notes: DAP is simpler than LSP, fewer message types
Debug adapters are separate binaries, similar architecture to language servers

user_stories.title: Breakpoints
acceptance_criteria: Given the cursor is on line 15, when the toggle-breakpoint shortcut is pressed, then a red marker appears in the gutter on line 15
Given line 15 has a breakpoint set, when the toggle-breakpoint shortcut is pressed again on line 15, then the red marker is removed
Given breakpoints are set on lines 10 and 20, when a debug session starts, then the DAP client sends setBreakpoints with both line numbers
Given the program is paused at line 10, when the editor renders, then line 10 has a distinct background color indicating the current execution point
Given a breakpoint is toggled during an active debug session, when the breakpoint changes, then the DAP client sends an updated setBreakpoints request immediately

user_stories.title: Stepping and Control
acceptance_criteria: Given the program is paused at a breakpoint on line 10, when Continue is pressed, then execution resumes until the next breakpoint or program exit
Given the program is paused at line 10, when Step Over is pressed, then execution advances to line 11 (or the next statement) and pauses
Given the program is paused at a function call on line 10, when Step Into is pressed, then execution enters the called function and pauses at its first line
Given the program is inside a function, when Step Out is pressed, then execution continues until the function returns and pauses at the caller
Given the program is running, when Stop is pressed, then the debug session terminates and the status bar returns to normal mode
Given the program is paused at line 20, when Step Over is pressed, then the execution line indicator moves from line 20 to the next line and the variable display refreshes

user_stories.title: Variable Inspection
acceptance_criteria: Given the program is paused at a breakpoint in a function with local variable x=42, when the variables panel renders, then it shows "x: 42"
Given a function has parameters name="hello" and count=3, when the variables panel renders, then both appear with their current values
Given a local variable "server" is a struct with fields Host and Port, when the user expands "server" in the variables panel, then it shows "Host: localhost" and "Port: 8080"
Given a variable "items" is a slice with 3 elements, when expanded, then it shows the index and value of each element
Given the program steps from line 10 to line 11 where x changes from 42 to 43, when the variable panel refreshes, then x shows the new value 43
